channel.title: The Grant Desk - Visual & Media Arts, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Visual & Media Arts, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Exploratorium Artist-in-Residence Program 2026-2027
item.description:
Organisation: Exploratorium

Location: San Francisco, California, United States. Residency is embedded within the Exploratorium; includes access to its facilities and staff.

Award: USD 15,000 annual stipend, plus support for travel, project management, and financial support for residency projects, and access to Exploratorium facilities and staff expertise.

Duration: Residencies typically unfold over two years, including an exploratory phase and a project-development phase (room for variance).

Deadline: 29 Jun 2026

Since its inception in 1974, the Exploratorium's Artist-in-Residence Program (AIR) has grown to include hundreds of artists and performers. The museum works with individuals and artist groups who are drawn to collaboration, interested in interdisciplinary dialogue, and open to developing new working methods. Projects have taken countless forms, such as multimedia performances, theatrical productions, animated filmmaking, immersive installations, walking tours, and online projects. The program allows artists to embed within the unique culture of the institution, affords access to a dynamic and distinct staff, and provides opportunities for cross-pollination with a diverse public. While the museum allows room for variance, residencies typically unfold over two years and include both an exploratory and a project-development phase. The AIR program is an opportunity for artists to begin their residency with a sense of curiosity and experimentation rather than a fully formed idea, and to work in a co-creative manner with the Exploratorium. Applicants should be inherently curious and deeply invested in inquiry as part of their practice. AIRs receive a USD 15,000 annual stipend, support for travel, project management and financial support for residency projects, and access to Exploratorium facilities and staff expertise. APPLICATION TIMELINE: the form opened 20 May 2026 with a no-cost early-bird period; a USD 35 regular application fee applies from 10 June 2026; last day to submit is 29 June 2026; finalists notified and interviews scheduled September 2026; notifications sent to all applicants November 2026. Apply for the 2026-2027 program at the Exploratorium AIR page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: NY20+ International Artist Residency (Chengdu, Sichuan, China, Ongoing)
item.description:
Organisation: NY20+ / Nongyuan Culture

Location: Chengdu, Sichuan, China

Award: No participation fee. Host covers accommodation and project materials. Artists pay their own travel and personal expenses. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 7 days; duration based on project plan.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

NY20+ International Artist Residency is an ongoing open residency in Chengdu, Sichuan, China, supported by Nongyuan Culture (founded 2008). Open internationally to artists, designers, art students, craftspeople, and groups from any discipline and at any career stage worldwide. The programme focuses on exploring Bashu Culture and Chengdu city, integrating traditional and modern elements, traditional Chinese handicrafts, and local heritage. PROVIDED: studio or workspace (private or shared) with basic art materials; furnished private or shared bedroom accommodation; meals (breakfast, lunch, dinner); technical support; cultural exchange opportunities; workshops, seminars, and exhibition or display opportunities. Artists are expected to donate one work to the residency upon completion. No fixed deadline; contact the organisation to confirm residency dates. Apply via the application form at http://www.ny20plus.com/en/h-col-158.html. Contact: ny20@cdnongyuan.com or +86 134 0287 6539.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: WSW Artist's Book Residency Grant
item.description:
Organisation: Women's Studio Workshop (WSW)

Location: Rosendale, NY, USA; in-person residency.

Award: $350/week stipend; up to $1,000 for materials; up to $250 for travel within the Continental US; free onsite housing; 24/7 studio access. No application fee.

Duration: 8-10 weeks; residency runs September through June, one year after application.

Deadline: 15 Nov 2026

Women's Studio Workshop (WSW) offers an eight- to ten-week residency for artists to produce a limited-edition artist's book. Working intensively in WSW's studios in Rosendale, NY, selected artists print and bind their own books; edition size must be between 50 and 100 copies. AMOUNT: $350/week stipend; up to $1,000 for materials; up to $250 for travel within the Continental US; free onsite housing; 24/7 studio access. WSW provides technical advice, training on new equipment, techniques, and materials, and production assistance. SALES CONTRACT: 20% of the edition goes to the artist; 20% (at least nine copies) stays with WSW for archival and institutional sales; 60% goes to general distribution and sale. If the artist sells through WSW, WSW takes a 30% commission plus 20% for marketing; the artist receives a 50% commission, paid annually around 30 June. WSW holds the right to sell distribution copies at a 20% discount to standing-order and repository institutions. WSW's books are held in over 450 collections worldwide, including the Library of Congress, Yale University, and Vassar College. An outside jury of artists and curators selects recipients. INDIGENOUS ARTISTS: applicants may check the appropriate box on the Submittable form to be considered for the BIMA Artist's Book Residency Grant for Indigenous Artists. APPLICATION MATERIALS (upload via Submittable): current resume; brief project description including media required (200-word maximum); materials budget form (Google Sheets, Excel, or PDF); up to ten images of recent work; image script (title, medium, dimensions, date). MAILED MATERIALS (to WSW at the address below): a physical book mock-up demonstrating how the book will be bound or function, actual size, with at least 1-2 fully realised spreads; and the shipping form (download and open the PDF locally). Mail to: Artist's Book Application, Women's Studio Workshop, PO Box 489, Rosendale, NY 12472. Applications open 1 August 2026; deadline 15 November 2026, 11:59pm EST; notification March 2027; residency runs September through June, one year after application. Apply via Submittable at https://wsworkshop.org/residencies/artists-book-residency-grant/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Freelands Foundation: Learning Through Making Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Freelands Foundation

Location: UK (England, Northern Ireland, Scotland, Wales); activity must occur within the United Kingdom.

Award: Up to £25,000. No application fee.

Duration: Projects of any length, taking place between 1 January and 31 December 2027. Funding provided no earlier than December 2026.

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

The Learning Through Making fund supports UK-based visual art organisations to bring opportunities for making and material exploration into their art education work. Freelands Foundation is looking for exploratory projects that embrace openness, collaboration, and iteration -- enabling organisations to work with materials in an expansive way, unpicking preconceptions about making and material literacy without predefined outcomes. ELIGIBLE ACTIVITIES: providing materials, transport, or access to expertise and facilities; action-research trials such as a new series of workshops or events developing material literacy; adding a making-focused element to existing activity. Projects may range from a few days to several months, but must take place between 1 January and 31 December 2027. AMOUNT: up to £25,000; funding provided no earlier than December 2026. ELIGIBILITY: UK-based visual art organisations run with a charitable purpose and for public benefit; eligible types include charities registered in England and Wales, Scotland, or Northern Ireland; exempt charities; galleries, museums, or organisations within a Local Authority or larger charitable body (e.g. universities); and Community Interest Companies or Community Benefit Societies meeting specific criteria (charitable purposes, asset lock, no dividend distribution, ability to segregate restricted funds). Organisations with a prior Freelands Foundation grant relationship are eligible; you may not apply for activity already funded by Freelands Foundation. Individuals are not eligible. HOW TO APPLY: download the Application Form, Application Guidance, and optional Access Form from freelandsfoundation.co.uk; email completed form to grants@freelandsfoundation.co.uk with subject line 'LTM Fund - Application'; one additional page of images or diagrams may be included. APPLICATION SUPPORT SESSIONS (online): 9 July 2026, 10:30am-12pm; 12 August 2026, 10:30am-12pm. KEY DATES: applications open 17 June 2026; close 11 September 2026 at 12pm; outcomes shared 23 November 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: AY-ROOP / Le Milieu: Reuse - (Re)making for Circus Residency 2026 (Rennes Métropole, France)
item.description:
Organisation: AY-ROOP

Location: Le Milieu, Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole, France. Applicants must legally reside in a Creative Europe country other than France and the French overseas territories (DROM-COM).

Award: Funded through Culture Moves Europe: EUR 900 mobility allowance per person plus flat-rate travel coverage; supplements for green mobility, visa fees, dependent children under 18, OCT/OR residents, and disability/accessibility needs (including possible full coverage of a sign-language interpreter). On-site single-room accommodation, lunches at Le Milieu canteen (Tuesday to Friday), shared kitchen, and technical support provided. No application fee.

Duration: 21-day residency, 28 November to 18 December 2026.

Deadline: 03 Jul 2026

AY-ROOP is launching an open call to select three circus artists and/or circus set designers/builders for a 21-day Culture Moves Europe research residency at Le Milieu (Saint-Jacques-de-la-Lande, Rennes Métropole) from 28 November to 18 December 2026. The residency is a research laboratory on reuse in contemporary circus: how everyday, locally available materials (paper/cardboard, plastics, lightweight metal, textiles, reclaimed wood, bicycle parts, etc.) can be used to create scenographic elements, sets, props and small apparatuses for juggling, acrobatics and balancing. The core principle is that objects are recreated locally from resources available in each territory rather than transported. AY-ROOP provides an equipped construction workshop, a rehearsal stage and circus tent, a guide to local reuse-sector contacts, and a full residency environment (accommodation, shared kitchen, working facilities); two mentors (a reuse specialist and a performing-arts professional) support the artists. PROFILES: circus artists interested in construction/scenography/DIY making, and builders/set designers working in circus; individual or group applications (max 3 people per group); emerging or established, any background. Accessible to artists with disabilities and accessibility needs. Working language English. No aerial apparatus or rigging: all work is ground-based. ELIGIBILITY: aged 18+ (no upper limit); legally resident in a Creative Europe country other than France and DROM-COM; available for the full residency. APPLY with a single PDF (1-page bio/CV, 5-page portfolio or video links, 1-page statement of intent, proof of residence). DEADLINE: 3 July 2026. Results announced 13 July 2026. Check the linked call PDF for full details.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Visual Anthropology Center (VAC): Residency Programme (Belgrade)
item.description:
Organisation: Visual Anthropology Center (VAC)

Location: Belgrade, Serbia (accommodation and studio in central Belgrade; additional workspace at Community Center KROV)

Award: EUR 1,000–1,500 per month (stipend, varies by project requirements), plus accommodation and studio space provided. No application fee.

Duration: Approximately 1–2 months; flexible packages depending on project needs

Deadline: 31 Dec 2027

Visual Anthropology Center (VAC) in Belgrade invites filmmakers, visual anthropologists, artists, and researchers to apply for its residency programme. The residency specialises in visual anthropology but is also open to individual and collaborative projects in experimental ethnography, creative documentary film, and other artistic mediums. Residents may apply with a project aimed at a public showing, or use the time and space to reflect, research, or write without a specific outcome. WHAT IS PROVIDED: accommodation and studio space in central Belgrade; additional workspace and community connection through Community Center KROV (an independent creative hub hosting lectures, events, and exhibitions); project guidance and mentorship from film professionals, ethnomusicologists, producers, artists, curators, and anthropologists; connections to local cultural and academic institutions, archives, galleries, and exhibition spaces; access to camera equipment, translators for fieldwork or editing, fixers, and project-specific services or materials; opportunity to exhibit a final project as an exhibition, installation, screening, workshop, or lecture. Eligible projects may be promoted through VAC's platforms and partner networks, including the Vizantrop Film Festival, for regional and international visibility. ELIGIBILITY: filmmakers, visual anthropologists, artists, and researchers; individual and collaborative projects welcome. Applications accepted on a rolling basis.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: OIF: Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods 2026 (Francophonie)
item.description:
Organisation: Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF)

Location: Francophone zone (OIF member states globally)

Award: Up to EUR 5,000 for artist mobility (travel, accommodation, visas, logistics). Up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs). Both can be combined. No application fee.

Duration: Residencies or tours of 5 days to 3 months. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026.

Deadline: 31 Aug 2026

The OIF Support for Artist Mobility and Circulation of Cultural Goods programme provides grants of up to EUR 5,000 for mobility (travel, accommodation, visa fees, logistics) and up to EUR 2,500 for circulation of cultural goods (excess baggage, freight, customs) within the Francophone zone; both can be combined. ELIGIBILITY: professional artists and established artistic collectives with a demonstrated connection to the Francophone space (by nationality, working language, or artistic collaborations), residing in an OIF member state, with minimum 2 years of experience in the cultural or artistic sector; must directly manage the project (not act as intermediaries); one application per individual per call. ELIGIBLE DISCIPLINES: live performance (music, theatre, dance, comedy, storytelling), cinema/audiovisual, literature/publishing, and visual arts. PRIORITY given to projects promoting gender equality, women artists, creators under 35, and South-South mobility. Projects must take place between 25 March and 30 November 2026. Multiple selection sessions throughout the year; call open until 31 August 2026. Information webinars: 11 March, 13 May, and 8 July 2026 at 3 PM Paris time. Contact: culture.dlc@francophonie.org. Apply at https://appelsaprojets.francophonie.org/fr/applications/soutien-a-la-circulation-des-biens-et-mobilite-des-artistes.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Seed Grant 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname; must have lived and worked there at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad, or plans taking place there, particularly encouraged.

Award: Fixed grant (no project budget required): EUR 5,000 for an individual, EUR 7,500 for a duo, EUR 10,000 for a collective. Paid in full within 2 weeks of award. No application fee.

Duration: Focused working period; plan starts at least 10 weeks after submission and runs a maximum of one year.

Deadline: 31 Oct 2026

The Stichting Stokroos Seed Grant gives individual makers, duos and collectives time and space to focus on their artistic development, for example a focused working period for research and experiment, or the development of a prototype, new work or methodology. There are two application rounds in 2026: 15 May to 30 June 2026 (now full) and 1 September to 31 October 2026; Stokroos considers a maximum of 60 eligible applications per period (or closes once the budget is reached) and supports around 50 applications per year. Eligibility: active as a visual artist, designer and/or craft practitioner (overlap possible); living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; at least 21 years old; you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme between 2018 and 2022, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. Duos and collectives may apply provided all participants meet the guidelines. The grant is a fixed amount with no budget required: EUR 5,000 (individual), EUR 7,500 (duo) or EUR 10,000 (collective). Plans can take place anywhere, start at least 10 weeks after submission and last a maximum of one year. You may apply for the Seed Grant once every two calendar years and receive it up to twice, and may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants. Apply via the online form (Dutch or English) with a portfolio PDF up to 20 pages/person, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages/person, and work/plan/collaboration descriptions up to 2,500 characters each. Stokroos aims to respond within 8 weeks; no appeal is possible. Next deadline: 31 October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Simone Veil Prize for European Jewish Cultural Heritage 2026
item.description:
Organisation: European Commission, Directorate-General for Education, Youth, Sport and Culture

Location: EU Member States and third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme

Award: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (five winners, one per category); up to 25 finalists recognized. No application fee.

Duration: For projects completed between January 2023 and January 2026

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Simone Veil Prize, administered by the European Commission, recognises outstanding projects preserving and promoting European Jewish cultural heritage. It is awarded across five categories: Shared Histories, Heritage for Dialogue, Living Heritage, Cross-border Cooperation and Shared Narratives, and Next Generation/Youth Engagement and Education. AWARD: €10,000 per Grand Prix winner (one per category, five in total); up to 25 finalists receive recognition. ELIGIBILITY: legal or natural persons (individuals or organisations) registered in EU Member States or third countries associated to the Creative Europe programme; projects must have been completed between January 2023 and January 2026. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026 at 23:59 CET. APPLY by submitting the completed application form by email to applications@simoneveilprize.eu.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Lee Ufan Arles: Prix Art & Environnement 2026 (Residency + Exhibition Prize)
item.description:
Organisation: Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain

Location: Arles, France (residency and exhibition)

Award: Funded residency (production space + accommodation) in Arles. No cash prize amount specified. No application fee.

Duration: 6–8 weeks residency in early 2027, followed by a solo summer 2027 exhibition

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

The Prix Art & Environnement (4th edition), awarded by Lee Ufan Arles and Maison Guerlain, supports an artist working at the intersection of art and the natural environment. The prize winner receives a funded 6–8 week production residency in Arles in early 2027, including production space, accommodation, and mentorship from Lee Ufan Arles staff, culminating in a solo summer 2027 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: open to all artists of any discipline and nationality, with no age or degree requirements; candidates must demonstrate at least one solo exhibition and one or more group exhibitions. DEADLINE: 31 July 2026. APPLY via the Google Form linked from the Lee Ufan Arles website.

Open call details

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Residency at Fundaziun Nairs 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Residency in Scuol, Switzerland (Fundaziun Nairs). Applicants must live and work in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad particularly encouraged.

Award: Fully supported stay (accommodation and meals paid directly to Fundaziun Nairs) plus an unrestricted living, travel and production budget of EUR 7,000. 80% paid before the residency, the remainder on receipt of a brief final report. No application fee.

Duration: Intensive three-month working period (July through September).

Deadline: 11 Sep 2026

Stichting Stokroos funds a three-month reflective residency at Fundaziun Nairs in Scuol, Switzerland, for one selected visual artist, designer or craft practitioner (overlap possible). Both emerging and established makers may apply. Stokroos covers full accommodation and meals directly with Fundaziun Nairs and provides a discretionary EUR 7,000 budget for living, travel and production. The application should include a proposal that engages specifically with the residency, its Alpine surroundings and/or its historical and cultural context. Eligibility: you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme in 2022 or earlier, or have an equivalent established practice, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); at least 21 years old; living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. Applications are individual only; duos and collectives cannot apply. Makers already selected by Fundaziun Nairs may still apply if they meet the criteria. You may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants, and must not have received a Seed Grant rejection in the previous calendar year. Apply via the online form (Dutch or English) with a portfolio PDF up to 20 pages, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages, and work/plan descriptions up to 2,500 characters each. Selection includes an introductory interview with up to three candidates; Stokroos aims to respond within 8 weeks. No appeal is possible. Application window: 1 to 11 September 2026. More information at nairs.ch.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Unruly Mirrors Open Call 2026 (East and Central Asia, Feminist Art)
item.description:
Organisation: Unruly Mirrors

Location: East and Central Asia (Kazakhstan, Japan, South Korea, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Uzbekistan, Mongolia)

Award: Financial support details not publicly confirmed at time of entry. No application fee confirmed.

Duration: Not publicly confirmed.

Deadline: 15 Jul 2026

Unruly Mirrors is an open call for artists based in Kazakhstan, Japan, South Korea, Mainland China, Hong Kong, Uzbekistan, or Mongolia. The project examines beauty practices in East and Central Asia as spaces of norm reproduction, intimacy, and feminist resistance. Financial support and full eligibility criteria were not publicly confirmed at time of entry (the website was unreachable). Deadline: 15 July 2026. Apply or inquire at https://unrulymirrors.com. NOTE: verify financial support and full details directly before applying.

Open call details

item.title: BFI Doc Society: Features Production Fund (UK, Documentary)
item.description:
Organisation: BFI Doc Society

Location: UK

Award: Up to £150,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Feature-length non-fiction films for theatrical release; rolling two-stage process (EOI then full application)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The BFI Doc Society Features Production Fund (delivered via BFI with Doc Society as delegate partner) supports UK filmmakers producing non-fiction feature films intended for theatrical release. Two strands: Directions (first- or second-time directors) and Momentum (established talent). AMOUNT: up to £150,000 non-recoupable grant (the total Doc Society contribution per project cannot exceed £150,000). ELIGIBILITY: UK-based filmmakers, both emerging and established directors; experienced and debut producers eligible; projects must meet at least three of six stated priorities: equity/diversity, audience impact, talent development, creative risk, UK-wide access, and environmental sustainability. PROCESS: two-stage — submit an Expression of Interest, then a Full Application if invited. Rolling intake year-round. Opened May 2026. APPLY via BFI Doc Society website. Contact: hellobfi@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Stichting Stokroos: Maker Initiatives Grant 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Stichting Stokroos

Location: Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname; must have lived and worked there at least one year. Applicants outside the Randstad, or plans taking place there, are particularly encouraged.

Award: Fixed grant of EUR 25,000 or EUR 40,000 (chosen depending on need). Maximum participant fee EUR 2,200/month (40-hour week); third-party hire capped at EUR 85/hour excl. VAT. Co-funding not required. No application fee.

Duration: Plan starts in the next calendar year (2027) and runs a maximum of two years.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Stichting Stokroos Maker Initiatives supports established visual artists, designers and craft practitioners (overlap possible), including duos and collectives, in developing and realising a multivocal artistic project or programme centred on a common concern, in collaboration with other makers or communities. There is one application round per year and Stokroos can support 4 to 6 proposals; a maximum of 40 eligible applications are considered per period. Eligibility: you completed your first subject-specific (vocational or higher) programme in 2020 or earlier, or have an equivalent established track record, or started without formal training (first presentation marks the start of your practice); at least 21 years old; living and working in the Kingdom of the Netherlands (including its Caribbean parts) or Suriname for at least one year; not currently enrolled in a study or post-academic programme. You must apply together with an artistic or creative partner (from professional makers to communities); duos and collectives must also have an external partner. You may submit only one application per year across all Stokroos grants, and must not have received a Seed Grant rejection in the previous calendar year. The grant is a fixed contribution of EUR 25,000 or EUR 40,000 depending on need. Plans can take place anywhere, start in the next calendar year and last a maximum of two years. Applications are submitted via the online form in Dutch or English (portfolio PDF up to 20 pages/person, optional video up to 10 minutes total, CV up to 2 pages/person, budget up to 3 pages, work/plan/collaboration descriptions up to 2,500 characters each). Stokroos aims to respond within 10 weeks; no appeal is possible. Application window: 20 to 31 July 2026. Questions: mail@stokroos.nl.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Pioneer Works: 2027 Open Call – Visual Arts and Music Residency (Brooklyn, NY)
item.description:
Organisation: Pioneer Works

Location: Pioneer Works, Red Hook, Brooklyn, New York, USA

Award: Visual Arts: USD 5,000 one-time award (USD 2,500 honorarium + USD 500/month stipend for 5 months). Music: USD 3,000 one-time award (USD 2,500 honorarium + USD 500/month stipend for 1 month). Free studio space and facilities access. No housing or travel provided. No application fee.

Duration: Visual Arts: 5 months. Music: 1 month. Cohort begins after jury decisions in late September 2026.

Deadline: 13 Jul 2026

Pioneer Works in Red Hook, Brooklyn invites applications from emerging visual artists and musicians for its 2027 residency cohort. 12 Visual Arts residents (5-month residency) and 12 Music residents (1-month residency) are selected each year through a two-phase jury process. WHAT IS PROVIDED: free studio space; access to facilities including risograph printer, darkroom, ceramics studio, large-format printer, 3D printers, thermobinder, and laser cutter; biweekly studio visits with curators, gallerists, critics, and artists; free access to all public programmes at Pioneer Works, including Second Sundays (monthly open studios and performances). FINANCIAL AWARD: USD 5,000 for Visual Arts residents; USD 3,000 for Music residents (each comprising a USD 2,500 honorarium plus USD 500/month for the residency duration); applicants' choice on how to spend it. Pioneer Works does not provide housing, travel, or visa sponsorship. ELIGIBILITY: open locally, nationally, and internationally; collectives may apply (resources shared); current students and programme alumni are not eligible. REVIEW PROCESS: phase one is internal scoring on technical ability, uniqueness of vision, and future potential (nontraditional career paths given special consideration); highest scorers advance to an external jury of art professionals and alumni who assess diversity of demographics and media, genuine studio need, and fit with Pioneer Works' public-facing culture. Jury convenes September 2026; all applicants notified by late September. Supported by Dalio Philanthropies, the Andy Warhol Foundation, NYC Department of Cultural Affairs, New York State Council on the Arts, and the National Endowment for the Arts. APPLY: via Pioneer Works website by 13 July 2026. Questions: residency@pioneerworks.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 (Documentary Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: Women Photograph (supported by Leica USA)

Location: Remote (international)

Award: Three grants of USD 10,000 each (USD 30,000 total). No application fee.

Duration: Project-based (new or in-progress photography projects).

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

Women Photograph + Leica Project Grants 2026 awards three grants of USD 10,000 each to support documentary photography projects. ELIGIBILITY: open to women, nonbinary, transgender, and intersex documentary photographers of any nationality; no membership required; no restrictions on camera equipment or career stage. Funding covers hard costs of reporting, photographers' creative fees, and any other expenses that support the production of new or in-progress still photography projects. Not intended for books, exhibitions, or workshops. Grantees retain full work ownership. One submission per applicant; applications in English (Google Translate acceptable); any use of generative AI must be disclosed. Applications opened 20 May 2026; deadline 30 June 2026 at 11:59 PM ET. Apply at https://app.picter.com/contests/bad7ea97-7b0d-4a9c-9e85-38e73529d62b/start.

Open call details

item.title: Nakanojo Biennale 2027: International Artist Open Call (Gunma Prefecture, Japan)
item.description:
Organisation: Nakanojo Biennale Executive Committee / Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture

Location: Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture, Japan

Award: Production grant provided (amount not specified publicly). No exhibition fee, no residency fee. Insurance subsidies available. Artists responsible for materials, food, transportation, and installation/removal costs. No application fee.

Duration: Exhibition: 18 September – 24 October 2027 (37 days). Production period: April–August 2027. Residency accommodation available from April 2027.

Deadline: 12 Jul 2026

Nakanojo Biennale 2027 (11th edition) is an international open call for artists of all genres — sculpture, painting, photography, video art, installation, performance, and more — to exhibit in Nakanojo Town, Gunma Prefecture, Japan. Theme: 'Echoing Green', inspired by William Blake's poem, exploring cycles of life, regeneration, and connection between past and future generations. Open internationally to individual artists and artist groups at all career stages; new participants are encouraged. A production grant is provided (amount not publicly specified); no exhibition or residency fees are charged. Residency accommodation available from April 2027. Staff support provided for venue selection and installation guidance. First-time participants in Japan must attend an on-site venue inspection; an online orientation on 5 September 2026 is required for all accepted artists. Deadline: 12 July 2026 at 23:59 JST (online). Apply via Google Form at https://forms.gle/SS8DaaGA6uwUKogj7. Full details at https://nakanojo-biennale.com/2027-open-call/english.html. Contact: office@nakanojo-biennale.com.

item.title: Samstag International Visual Arts Scholarships - Class of 2027
item.description:
Organisation: Samstag Museum of Art, University of South Australia

Location: Australia (applicants must be Australian citizens or permanent residents); 12-month placement at an international institution of the scholar's choice.

Award: AUD $75,000 tax-exempt stipend; institutional fees for one academic year of study (where applicable); return airfares; and travel and health insurance. No application fee.

Duration: 12 months overseas at an international learning institution.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

The Samstag International Visual Arts Scholarships are annual scholarships enabling Australian artists to develop their artistic capacities through a dedicated year of practice-based learning at an international institution of their choice. AMOUNT: AUD $75,000 tax-exempt stipend; institutional fees for one academic year (where applicable); return airfares; and travel and health insurance. ELIGIBILITY: Australian citizens or permanent residents aged 18 or over who are either (a) currently enrolled in a practice-led visual arts program at an Australian institution of higher education, having successfully completed at least two years of an undergraduate or postgraduate degree; or (b) graduates of a practice-led visual arts undergraduate or postgraduate qualification at an Australian institution of higher education (graduates of more than five years' standing are now eligible following 2023 guideline updates). PROCESS: applicants must create an account in the online portal, download the Samstag Microsoft PowerPoint template, read the Class of 2027 Guidelines, and complete and submit the online application form. Accounts can be created before the deadline and applications saved for later submission. Prior applicants (Classes of 2024, 2025, or 2026) can reuse the same account. Deadline: 30 June 2026, midnight Australian Central Standard Time (ACST). Apply at https://samstag.adelaide.edu.au/scholarship/apply/.

Open call details

item.title: Async Museum: Open Call for Web-Based Art 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Async Museum

Location: Online (independent digital space); open to artists worldwide.

Award: Exhibition and visibility opportunity, NOT a paid grant: selected web-based artworks are presented in Async Museum's independent online space. No artist fee is stated.

Duration: Rolling open call (exhibition in Async Museum's online space)

Deadline: 01 Dec 2026

Async Museum is an independent digital space dedicated to web art with a distinctive point of view, existing to discover artists who use the web as their primary medium and to recognise their work as a new aesthetic. Its core philosophy is that the web browser is a twenty-first century canvas. Async Museum is officially seeking submissions of web-based artworks, welcoming both established practitioners and emerging voices who are pushing the boundaries of what the next generation of art can be. NOTE: this is an exhibition opportunity for web-based art rather than a paid grant; no artist fee is stated. To share your practice and submit your work, visit https://asyncmuseum.com/submit. item.title: ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026: Open Call (Vienna, Austria)
item.description:
Organisation: Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography

Location: Academy of Fine Arts, Vienna, Austria

Award: EUR 250 cash award per selected winner + EUR 150 travel allowance. Grand Prix winner receives an additional EUR 250, a solo exhibition, and a free hotel stay. Submission fee: EUR 20 per project.

Duration: Exhibition at Academy of Fine Arts Vienna; announcement August 2026, public reveal September 2026.

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

ROTLICHT Festival for Analog Photography 2026, organised by Zigutamve – Association for Contemporary Photography, selects 20 projects for exhibition at the Academy of Fine Arts Vienna. Theme: 'Anthropocene: Hybrid Realities' – exploring the tension between human ecological dominance and environmental fragility. Each selected winner receives: exhibition in a 1,300+ m² venue, publication in the official festival catalogue (2 copies), EUR 250 cash award, EUR 150 travel allowance, festival pass, and free registration for Photodays Rovinj 2027. Grand Prix winner additionally receives a solo exhibition, an extra EUR 250, a free hotel stay, and a catalogue cover feature. ELIGIBILITY: open to any individual aged 16 or older, at any career stage, internationally; submissions must include at least one analog photographic process; works must have been taken within the last 5 years (2021-2026); no AI-generated or AI-enhanced work (unless deliberately thematic). Files must be JPEG, max 5 MB, minimum 2000px on longest side, 300 dpi. Jury selects winners by late July 2026. Deadline: 30 June 2026. NOTE: EUR 20 submission fee per project applies. Apply at https://www.rotlicht-festival.at/open-call-submission/.

Location: International (Francophone zone and Arab world)

Award: EUR 10,000 (funded equally by OIF and ALECSO). No application fee.

The Ibn Khaldoun – Léopold Sédar Senghor Translation Prize (19th edition, 2026) is awarded jointly by the Organisation internationale de la Francophonie (OIF) and ALECSO to recognise excellence in literary and humanities/social sciences translation between French and Arabic in either direction. Cash award: EUR 10,000. ELIGIBILITY: open to individual translators, universities and higher education institutions, research centres, national associations and unions, and publishing houses from the Arab world and Francophone space. Works must be literary or humanities/social sciences texts translated between French and Arabic (either direction), published within 3 years prior to the application year, and must be a first translation of the original. Self-published or photocopied works excluded. One work maximum per candidate. Applications submitted exclusively electronically; required materials: CV, presentation of translation work, copies of both translated and original texts. Deadline: 30 June 2026. item.title: Amarte Fonds: Development and Project Contributions (rolling)
item.description:
Organisation: Amarte Fonds

Location: Netherlands and the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). Applicants must be based there.

Award: Development contribution up to EUR 2,750; project contribution up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent paid in advance, remainder after the report is approved. No application fee.

Duration: Project-based; rolling decisions.

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Amarte Fonds is a private Dutch foundation that supports the arts, with rolling (continuous) contributions for visual artists and independent curators. There are two contribution types: a development contribution of up to EUR 2,750 and a project contribution of up to EUR 5,500. Up to 80 percent is paid in advance, with the remainder after the final report is approved. Applicants must be based in the Netherlands or the Caribbean part of the Kingdom (the ABC and SSS islands). The fund also supports work across theatre, film, music, literature and interdisciplinary projects through specific programmes. There is no fixed deadline and no application fee; decisions are relatively fast. Apply year-round via the online portal.

item.title: eidolon Grant 2026 (Vernacular Photography)
item.description:
Organisation: eidolon

Location: International; open worldwide.

Award: A total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed across two application categories. No application fee.

Duration: Annual grant; supported projects contribute to the institution's program in the coming year

Deadline: 30 Sep 2026

The eidolon Grant is an international programme, presented annually, for projects that explore, promote and conserve vernacular photography. It is open to artists, academics, professionals, researchers, collectors and vernacular photography enthusiasts whose past work and proposed project is centred around the image heritage of everyday photography. The grant aims to identify phenomena, collections, histories, practices and trends within vernacular photography, offering new interpretations and analyses, and thematising both photographic heritages and contemporary photographic practices. Applications can be made under two categories, with a total of EUR 25,000 to be distributed. Each chosen project will contribute to the enrichment of eidolon's program in the coming year. The deadline for submission is 30 September 2026 at 11:59 p.m. Apply via https://everydayphotography.org/centre/grant-2026.

Open call details

Award: Up to £25,000 non-recoupable grant. No application fee.

Duration: Short films 5–40 minutes; up to 13 projects funded per year

Deadline: 01 Jul 2026

BFI Doc Society's Made of Truth fund supports early-career UK filmmakers making non-fiction live-action or animated short films of 5–40 minutes. Up to 13 projects are funded per annual round. AMOUNT: up to £25,000 per project. ELIGIBILITY: early-career directors and producers who are UK residents; teams must have at least two people (a producer and a director); directors cannot have received a commercial distribution deal for a non-fiction feature in the last five years; a reasonable proportion of early-career crew is required. item.title: MAK Center: Artists and Architects in Residence 2027/2028 (MAK Schindler Scholarship, Los Angeles)
item.description:
Organisation: MAK Center for Art and Architecture, Los Angeles

Location: Los Angeles, California, United States (Mackey Apartments). Residents must be present in L.A. for the full six-month term.

Award: Six-month MAK Schindler Scholarship residency with accommodation provided at the Mackey Apartments. The open call does not state a stipend, travel or honorarium figure; confirm any additional financial support directly with the MAK Center. No application fee stated.

Duration: Six months. Residency terms run October to March or April to September; 2027/2028 cycle.

Deadline: 12 Aug 2026

The MAK Center for Art and Architecture awards Artists and Architects in Residence scholarships (the MAK Schindler Scholarship) to independent projects exploring the relation between art and architecture. Projects are chosen for their ability to critically examine current trends in art, architecture and society; to make a compelling connection with Los Angeles as the place of implementing or continuing the applicant's own work; and to demonstrate a conceptual and experimental approach. Emphasis is placed on the L.A. location, and residents' projects often reflect, incorporate and address specific facets of the city and its social fabric. Recipients are encouraged to take part in the MAK Center's activities and to attend meetings, workshops and project discussions with other artists and students. ELIGIBILITY: freelance artists; fine arts or architecture students at a university who have completed the '2. Studienabschnitt'; or architects or artists who have recently graduated. Teams of up to three are eligible if all members practice together as an artist group or collective of architects, are all present in L.A. for the six months, and name a contact responsible for the team. HOUSING: the Mackey Apartments are made available to scholarship holders only; family members, relatives and friends may be accommodated for a maximum of two weeks during the term, permitted in advance by Beth Stryker, Director of the MAK Center. Eligibility is excluded if the applicant has been or will be granted another scholarship in the USA immediately before or after the MAK scholarship, or if the data form is incomplete or the required portfolio is missing. Residency terms run October to March and April to September. Deadline 12 August 2026. Apply: https://www.makcenter.org/apply-mackey

Open call details

item.title: FUTURES Photography: 2027 Residency Open Call
item.description:
Organisation: FUTURES Photography

Location: Futures Hub, Amsterdam, Netherlands; ISSP, Riga, Latvia; Capa Center, Budapest, Hungary

Award: EUR 1,000 stipend; accommodation provided. No application fee.

Duration: Minimum 1 month; location-specific dates in 2027 (Amsterdam: Jan, Feb, Apr, May, Jun, Sep, Oct, Nov, Dec; Riga: May; Budapest: Apr)

Deadline: Closed 22 Jun 2026

ELIGIBILITY: Open exclusively to current FUTURES artists (existing members of the FUTURES Photography platform); the application is free of charge. CONCEPT: The residency supports emerging photographers with time, space, and resources to develop new work, with a strong emphasis on research and artistic process. Artists are invited to explore the intersection of Nature, Environment, and Technology - projects should challenge perceptions of human and ecological relationships, technological advancement, and environmental responsibility. An interdisciplinary approach is encouraged, and each residency culminates in a public event where artists share their work and engage with diverse audiences. LOCATIONS: Three partner venues host residencies at different times in 2027. (1) Futures Hub, Amsterdam (global FUTURES HQ): a 60 sqm ground-floor studio with heating, electricity, and private entrance; live-work space with double folding bed, private bathroom, washing machine, and small kitchen; sliding door separates work and living areas; access to a shared atrium for large-scale works, performances, and presentations; communal rooftop terrace; bed linens and towels provided; available January, February, April, May, June, September, October, November, December 2027. (2) ISSP, Riga: private bedroom with working space in a shared city-centre apartment with two other artists; spacious shared living room and fully equipped kitchen; access to ISSP office facilities including printer, scanner, ISSP photography library, and studio lights; available May 2027. (3) Capa Center, Budapest: fully furnished private room in a communal apartment at Art Quarter Budapest; 400 sqm shared studio in a postindustrial area; services to integrate residents into the local Budapest art scene and establish professional connections; option to organise a presentation at Capa Centre in downtown Budapest; access to Capa Centre office facilities (printer, scanner); available April 2027. PRESENCE: minimum 90% presence required throughout the residency period. ENGAGEMENT: residents contribute to a Visual Diary (one Instagram carousel and two Stories, in collaboration with FUTURES), a short Video Portrait documenting their process, a public event such as an open studio or workshop (open studios on the last Wednesday of the month), and a written interview with a local writer published on the FUTURES website. FINANCIAL SUPPORT: EUR 1,000 stipend to cover living expenses, materials, and travel; accommodation is provided at all locations. Residents are responsible for arranging travel documents, health and travel insurance, liability insurance, and any visa requirements (support provided where needed). APPLY: via the FUTURES website; application is free and open only to FUTURES artists. TIMELINE: deadline 22 June 2026 at 23:59 Amsterdam time; results announced July 2026. Contact info@futures-photography.com for questions.

Open call details

item.title: The Cooper Prize 2026 (16th Street Foundation and Company 16, Melbourne)
item.description:
Organisation: 16th Street Foundation and Company 16

Location: Melbourne, Australia (international eligibility)

Award: AUD 20,000 prize to winner, plus consideration for a full commission and production of the winning work. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. No application fee.

Duration: Prize (one-time award); winning work may proceed to full commission and production.

Deadline: Closed 19 Jun 2026

The Cooper Prize 2026 is an international playwriting prize administered by 16th Street Foundation and Company 16 in Melbourne, Australia. AUD 20,000 is awarded to the winning playwright, plus consideration for a full commission and professional production. All finalists receive professionally directed public readings by professional actors. Open internationally to published and unpublished playwrights at any career stage with no geographic restrictions. Submissions must respond to thematic prompts drawn from Victor Frankl's 'Man's Search for Meaning' and Dostoevsky's 'The Brothers Karamazov', exploring themes including doubt versus faith, the meaning of suffering, and the power of truth. Application requires: (1) a one-page document covering motivation for writing, issues to explore, philosophical perspective or worldview, and a synopsis or treatment; (2) ten pages of the play manuscript. Judging panel led by Iain Sinclair and Harry Haynes. Deadline: 19 June 2026. Apply via JotForm at https://form.jotform.com/260408855140858.

Open call details

item.title: Sandberg Instituut: Call for Proposals, Temporary Department 2027-2029 (Head of Department, The Archive)
item.description:
Organisation: Sandberg Instituut

Location: Amsterdam, Netherlands; applicants must be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate for the duration of the programme.

Award: Freelance position as Head of the Temporary Department, approx. two days per week over two academic years (September 2027 to July 2029), plus allocated hours for curriculum development (October 2026 to July 2027). Approximate daily rate EUR 553.60. The department is also supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a dedicated department coordinator plus administrative support, and institutional infrastructure and guidance. A modest fee is provided for the second-round interview stage. No application fee.

Duration: Two-year Temporary Department, September 2027 to July 2029 (curriculum development from October 2026)

Deadline: 05 Jul 2026

Sandberg Instituut invites artists, designers, researchers, makers and educators to propose a Temporary Department, taking on the role of Head of Department for the period September 2027 to July 2029. THEME, THE ARCHIVE: for this edition the institute seeks proposals that engage with the archive as a contemporary artistic, social, political and pedagogical framework. Increasingly recognised as a site of power, selection, memory, erasure and imagination, the archive is approached here not as a static collection but as a living, contested and generative process to be activated, reinterpreted and transformed through contemporary practice, and as a material and methodological field to work with, against and through. Possible starting points include: archives as sites of resistance, repair and restitution; embodied, oral and performative archives; collective and community-based archives; digital, algorithmic and AI-driven processes; counter-archives and practices of refusal; speculative archives and future-oriented forms of memory; creative practices that activate, reinterpret or transform existing archives; and the politics of preservation, access and knowledge production. Proposals should articulate a compelling approach to the archive plus a clear, connected pedagogical vision for how collective research unfolds over two years while supporting individual trajectories; partnerships with external organisations (museums, social archives, artist-led initiatives, community organisations and research institutions) are encouraged. FORMAT: Temporary Departments are full-time two-year master's studies bringing a cohort of students and tutors together around a specific field of inquiry; successful completion leads to a Master in Fine Arts and Design accredited by the NVAO. POSITION: the freelance Head of Department role is equivalent to approx. two days per week over the two academic years plus curriculum-development hours (October 2026 to July 2027), at an approximate daily rate of EUR 553.60, supported by a budget for core tutors and guest contributors, a department coordinator with administrative support, and institutional infrastructure. WHO CAN APPLY: applicants should have teaching/education experience; be able to develop and coordinate a two-year MA curriculum rooted in their own research and practice; have strong communication and leadership skills; have cross-disciplinary collaborative experience; be committed to an inclusive, supportive learning environment responsive to diverse access needs; be based in the Netherlands or willing to relocate; hold an established independent professional practice registered with the Dutch Chamber of Commerce (KVK); and, if applying from outside the EU, hold a valid Dutch residence and work permit for the appointment. SELECTION (two rounds): first-round submissions to td@sandberg.nl by 17:00 on Sunday 5 July 2026 (a max. 500-word department proposal, a max. 200-word networks and collaborations statement, and a max. 2-page CV); first-round notification 15 July 2026; interviews with shortlisted candidates 16 September 2026. Questions: td@sandberg.nl.

Open call details

item.title: Treehouse NDSM: Open Call, Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture) Exhibition 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Treehouse NDSM

Location: Treehouse NDSM Pavilion, NDSM, Amsterdam-Noord, Netherlands; particular interest in artists from Amsterdam Noord.

Award: An artist fee is provided to all participating artists. Selected works are exhibited on the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion. No application fee. (Artist fee amount not stated.)

Duration: Exhibition 17 September to 11 October 2026

Deadline: 28 Jun 2026

Treehouse NDSM invites artists to apply for its next exhibition, 'Come Back to Your ___ (Nature/Culture)', running 17 September to 11 October 2026. In the context of current ecological crises, the exhibition takes the constant interaction between culture and nature as its starting point, noting how societies shape the land (from Japanese gardens to post-Soviet geometric parks, Dutch dikes and polders, and Indigenous Australian cultural burning) and how environments shape societies in turn. Artists are invited to reflect on questions such as: how do nature and culture share the same ground? How has your community shaped the land, and how has the land shaped your community? The exhibition is part of Art Park, a series launched at Treehouse in 2023 that reflects on urban nature, particularly in Amsterdam-Noord. The organisers welcome projects that do not offer answers but dig into questions and leave room for uncertainty, across media including performance, painting, making, installation and video. SELECTION: the committee (artist Alina Bielun and artistic coordinator Mulan Go) will select up to ten artworks for the 100 m2 mezzanine of Treehouse NDSM's Pavilion, considering both relevance to the theme and the chosen medium, with particular interest in the voices of artists from Amsterdam Noord (temporary or performative works that meaningfully complement the exhibition are also welcome). An artist fee is provided to all participants. Treehouse NDSM is not a traditional gallery but a multidisciplinary incubator offering 100 affordable studios, exhibition spaces and a collaborative artistic community. Submission deadline: 28 June 2026. Apply via the online form.

Open call details

item.title: Circolo del Design: Earthrise 26 Residency for Young Designers 2026 (Turin)

No application fee.

Earthrise 26 (Design for a Living Planet), the annual cultural initiative by Circolo del Design, will unfold around clay and earthenware, approached not as functional objects but as narrative, spatial and speculative matter. One of the projects featured in the exhibition will be developed through the Earthrise 26 Residency (5 October to 3 December 2026), a unique opportunity for an under-35 international designer to create and experiment within an interdisciplinary context. Under the mentorship of Designregio Kortrijk and BASE Milano among others, the selected resident will collaborate with local designers, artisans and interdisciplinary experts in Turin to produce an original body of work exhibited as part of the Earthrise 26 exhibition. ELIGIBILITY: under 35; a demonstrated international practice through research, collaborations, cultural exchange or mobility within Europe; well-structured project planning with clear goals; thematic relevance aligned with the objectives of Earthrise 2026; and an experimental and/or innovative approach. WHAT IS OFFERED: a designer's fee of EUR 1,500 (VAT included), travel and accommodation for both phases, a public transport pass, up to EUR 3,000 (VAT included) for project production, a dedicated 30 sqm workspace, and mentorship and technical/logistical/organisational support. item.title: AIR Taipei: International Artist Residency 2027 (Taipei Artist Village & Treasure Hill)
item.description:
Organisation: Taipei Culture Foundation (AIR Taipei, operating Taipei Artist Village and Treasure Hill Artist Village)

Location: Taipei, Taiwan; on-site residency.

Award: Funding varies by track. INTERNATIONAL RESIDENCY (open to international artists): free accommodation and studio space plus administrative and project support; no cash artist grant. PUPPETRY RESIDENCY (Treasure Hill x Puppetry Art Center, open to international artists): NT$20,000 (around USD 620) subsidy plus accommodation and studio. OVERSEAS RESIDENCY VISIT (Taiwanese artists only): NT$100,000 (around USD 3,100) creative grant plus economy-class roundtrip airfare. No application fee.

Duration: Approx. 6 weeks to 3 months depending on category (International Residency roughly 8 to 12 weeks), in 2027

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

AIR Taipei is the international artist-in-residence program run by the Taipei Culture Foundation across the Taipei Artist Village and Treasure Hill Artist Village. The 2027 open call welcomes artists, curators, researchers, performers, writers, cultural practitioners and interdisciplinary creators working in visual art, performance, music, architecture, literature, curatorial practice, cultural studies and interdisciplinary arts. TRACKS AND ELIGIBILITY: international applicants can apply to the International Residency (free accommodation and studio space plus administrative and project support, roughly 8 to 12 weeks, no cash artist grant) and to the Treasure Hill x Puppetry Art Center Project Residency (NT$20,000, around USD 620, subsidy plus accommodation and studio). The Overseas Residency Visit, which carries a NT$100,000 (around USD 3,100) creative grant and economy-class roundtrip airfare, is open to Taiwanese artists only and does not apply to international applicants. Residency length runs approximately 6 weeks to 3 months depending on the category and host institution, taking place in 2027. International applicants must submit a resume and residency plan in English or Chinese. APPLY at https://air-artistvillage.org.tw/ before 11:59:59 (Taipei time, late morning) on Tuesday 30 June 2026. Questions: air@artistvillage.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CreArt 3.0: Residency at Chalet Lecoq 2026 (Clermont-Ferrand)
item.description:
Organisation: City of Clermont-Ferrand (CreArt network)

Location: Chalet Lecoq, Jardin Lecoq, Clermont-Ferrand, France.

Award: Residency grant EUR 3,000 per artist for the two-month residency (EUR 1,500/month), plus EUR 600 production costs per artist. For European (CreArt network) artists: reimbursement of round-trip travel up to EUR 500, and accommodation (a two-bedroom apartment) plus a fully-equipped workspace; the Clermont-Ferrand artist receives the workspace. No application fee.

Duration: Two-month residency, 26 October to 20 December 2026

Deadline: Closed 21 Jun 2026

As part of the CreArt network, the City of Clermont-Ferrand in France is organising a two-month residency for three visual artists at Chalet Lecoq, selecting two artists from the CreArt network and one from Clermont-Ferrand. Both central and atypical, set in the middle of a public garden (the Jardin Lecoq, a park in the heart of the city), the Chalet Lecoq is the former janitor's house of the park and invites reflection on the relationship between nature and the urban, in a place frequented by residents of all ages. It offers a privileged setting with a two-bedroom apartment and a fully-equipped workspace, and welcomes international artists in residency throughout the year. An end-of-residency presentation may be organised in discussion with the hosted artists. CONDITIONS: residency grant of EUR 3,000 per artist (EUR 1,500/month); EUR 600 production costs per artist; for European artists, reimbursement of round-trip travel up to EUR 500; accommodation and workspace for the two European artists, and workspace for the Clermont-Ferrand artist. ELIGIBILITY: artists born or resident in one of the following CreArt cities (Kaunas, Lithuania; Liepaja, Latvia; Skopje, North Macedonia; Aveiro, Portugal; Valladolid, Spain; Lublin, Poland; Venice, Italy; Rouen, France; Ceske Budejovice, Czech Republic; Oulu, Finland; Regensburg, Germany); members of the Croatian Association of Visual Artists (HDLU); and Ukrainian artists through cooperation with the Lviv Art Council 'Dialog'. APPLY in English (a single PDF with ID/passport and residency certificate if required, a one-page CV, a portfolio of max 10 pages/10 images, and a one-page project proposal) via the CreArt artists' platform. A jury of Clermont-Ferrand contemporary art professionals selects the artists, with particular attention to work that may reveal forms of connection. Deadline: 21 June 2026 (midnight); selected candidates announced 3 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: PLAY26 Creative Gaming Festival: Open Call for Games & Playful Media Works
item.description:
Organisation: PLAY - Creative Gaming Festival

Location: Hamburg, Germany; international submissions welcome (with a Focus Hamburg feature for artists and developers based in Hamburg).

Award: Exhibition and awards opportunity, NOT a paid grant: selected works are showcased to an international festival audience. Awards include the Most Creative Game Award and an Audience Award (all games in the festival exhibition are eligible, voted on by festival visitors). No artist fee is stated.

Duration: Festival 2026 (exhibition and awards show)

Deadline: 28 Jun 2026

PLAY is all about the creative use of digital games, combining this culture with media art, discourse and education. This year's theme, 'Hey, listen! (Music & Emotion)', relates to mixtape culture and the sharing of bonds and feelings with the people you like and love. The open call invites artists and developers to submit games or playful media works (including alt.ctrl, interactive theatre and performances) for one of three strands: the Creative Gaming Awards (works that let players get creative in or with them, including the Most Creative Game Award); the core exhibition 'Hey, listen!' (works fitting this year's Music & Emotion theme); and Focus Hamburg (a Local Artists Feature for artists and developers based in Hamburg). All games shown in the festival exhibition are eligible for the Audience Award, chosen by festival visitors and honoured at the Awards Show. Selected projects are showcased to an international audience. NOTE: this is a festival exhibition and awards open call rather than a paid grant; no artist fee is stated. The Call for Games & Playful Media Works is open until 28 June 2026 (Calls for Talks and Workshops follow). Submit via https://www.playfestival.de/en/play26-submissions-are-open.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: SVĚTOVA 1: As We Grow V (Educational & Research Program) 2026
item.description:
Organisation: SVĚTOVA 1

Location: Prague, Czech Republic; participants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program.

Award: Scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200). Free 14-day educational programme; work exhibited at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks with production and PR support. NO accommodation provided (funding restrictions). No application fee.

Duration: 14 days, 7 to 20 September 2026

Deadline: 30 Jun 2026

SVĚTOVA 1 invites early-career artists and cultural organizers to apply for the fifth edition of its educational and research programme As We Grow. This year the course is led by artist and mythopoet András Cséfalvay, Associate Professor at the Academy of Fine Arts in Bratislava. The 14-day project (7 to 20 September 2026) culminates in a group exhibition. WHAT YOU GET: a free 14-day educational experience; a scholarship of CZK 5,000 (around EUR 200); a rich working environment with on-site interaction with the leading artist, scholars and other artists; active involvement in the As We Grow research and event programming; organizational and professional support; and exhibition of your work at SVĚTOVA 1 for at least six weeks, with production and PR support before, during and after the show. SVĚTOVA 1 actively encourages queer and BIPoC people who have migrated to or taken refuge in Prague to apply. ELIGIBILITY: applicants must be physically present in Prague for the entire program, have essential spoken English, and commit to the whole 14 days after acceptance. Note: accommodation cannot be offered due to funding restrictions. APPLY via the online Notion form with a portfolio of 3 works, a CV (max 2 pages), and either a 2-3 minute video or a 250-word motivation letter (may include images) outlining how your lived experience and interest align with the course. Deadline: 30 June 2026 at midnight; results announced by 15 July 2026. Questions: tomas@svetova1.cz.

Open call details
